skill.name: verification-before-completion
skill.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
skill.summary: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - req...
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: This is a meta-skill providing verification guidelines. The static scanner flagged 13 external_commands patterns and 1 weak crypto pattern, but evaluation shows these are FALSE POSITIVES: the skill contains instructions for AI agents to run verification commands (tests, linters, builds) as text examples - not actual executable code. No cryptographic code exists. System/network reconnaissance flags refer to standard DevOps terms (VCS, git diff). This skill promotes honest verification practices and poses no security risk.

risk_factor_evidence.confidence_reasoning: Skill contains text instructions, not executable code. References to commands are examples for the AI to follow.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flag
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged line 3 for weak crypto algorithm. Investigation shows this is a FALSE POSITIVE - the line contains only description text about verification workflows with no cryptographic code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms no cryptographic algorithms exist in this file - only plain text instructions.

medium_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Flag
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged lines 71 and 111 for 'system reconnaissance'. These references are to standard DevOps concepts like 'Agent delegation' and 'VCS diff' - not actual system scanning.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms 'Agent' and 'VCS diff' refer to AI agent delegation and version control - standard software development terminology, not reconnaissance tools.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Flag
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged line 130 for 'network reconnaissance'. This refers to 'communication' about completion status, not network scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line discusses communication patterns for reporting completion, not network activity or reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: This skill prevents AI agents from making false completion claims by requiring actual verification evidence before reporting success. It ensures tests pass, builds succeed, and work is genuinely complete.
actual_capabilities: Instructs AI to run fresh verification commands before claiming success
Provides patterns for test verification with actual output evidence
Lists common failure modes where agents claim completion prematurely
Includes rationalization prevention table for self-deception
Defines red flags that indicate premature completion claims
Creates verification checklist for requirements validation
limitations: Does not execute verification commands itself - guides AI behavior
Relies on AI following instructions, cannot enforce compliance
Does not provide specific test frameworks or tooling
Requires user to define what verification means for their project

use_cases.title: Test Verification Before Commit
use_cases.description: Before committing code, require the AI to run test commands and show actual pass/fail counts from fresh execution.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers using Claude or Codex

use_cases.title: Build Verification Before PR
use_cases.description: Prevent premature PR creation by requiring build command output showing exit code 0 before claiming success.

use_cases.title: Regression Test Validation
use_cases.description: Ensure regression tests follow red-green pattern: pass after fix, fail without fix, pass after restore.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Verification Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Before claiming this work is complete, run the verification command and show me the actual output. Do not claim success without evidence.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when AI is about to declare a task finished

prompt_templates.title: Test Verification Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full test suite and report: total tests, passed, failed, and the exact error messages for any failures. Show the exit code.

prompt_templates.title: Build Verification Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the build command and show the exit code. Report any compilation errors or warnings from the output.

prompt_templates.title: Requirements Checklist Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the original requirements. For each requirement, state: met/unmet/gap. Cite specific evidence from the code or test output.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when verifying all requirements are satisfied

output_examples.output:
Running test suite...

Results: 42 tests, 42 passed, 0 failed
Exit code: 0

All tests pass.

Running build command...

> npm run build
Exit code: 0
Build completed successfully.

Output length: 2.3MB

Running reproduction test...

Original failing input: {id: 123}
Expected: Error thrown
Actual: Error thrown

Bug is confirmed fixed.

best_practices: Always run fresh verification commands - never trust cached or previous results
Read the full output including exit codes before making any claims
Express actual status with evidence, not confidence or assumptions
anti_patterns: Claiming completion based on agent success reports without independent verification
Using words like 'should', 'probably', 'seems to' before verification
Relying on partial checks like linter passing when full build verification is needed

faq.question: Does this skill run tests for me?
faq.answer: No. This skill instructs the AI to run verification commands. You must configure the actual test and build commands for your project.

faq.question: Can I use this with any testing framework?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is framework-agnostic. It provides guidelines for verification regardless of your test tooling.

faq.question: What if verification takes a long time?
faq.answer: The skill emphasizes that skipping verification to save time creates false confidence. The cost of verification is less than the cost of rework from false completion.

faq.question: Does this work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It guides the AI's behavior regardless of the specific tool.

faq.question: How is this different from just running tests?
faq.answer: Running tests is insufficient. This skill requires: fresh execution, full output review, exit code checking, and evidence-based claims. Partial or cached results do not qualify.

faq.question: Can this prevent all false completion claims?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidelines but cannot enforce compliance. It relies on the AI following instructions. Human oversight remains important.
